title: salix plectilis
intro: Salix plectilis, also known as the Mexican willow or willow-leaf willow, is a species of willow tree native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Salix plectilis include reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of the common cold and flu, as well as treating skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of Salix plectilis include teas and salves made from the bark and leaves, which can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and internally to support immune function.
